content.text: Excerpted from the Contractor Field Manual, Capability Schedule. Contractors are assessed across twelve competencies. Assessment is continuous. The field administers it.

A skill is a trained competency. When you attempt a task, your tier in the relevant skill supplies your accuracy dice, the skill's stat supplies your bonus, and the difficulty sets the DC. The dice ladder (T0 untrained through T4 master), the DC ladder, outcome bands, flash of brilliance, advantage, and contests all live on The Two Rolls. This page owns what each skill covers and what its tiers grant.

content.label: TIERS GRANT DICE + FEATURES
content.text: A skill tier never adds a modifier to the roll. Tiers grant bigger dice and features — verbs and outcome control. No feature is a roll modifier, no feature is damage, combat utilities cost at most 1 AP, and no feature grants more than +1 of any capped currency, conditionally.
At each of tiers 1 through 3 you gain the tier's dice and pick one feature from that tier's pair. T4 grants the dice and its mastery feature outright.
A skill makes you able; a profession makes it pay. Mod Artisan lets you install your own mods at material cost — the Tinkerer has clients. What you are in the economy lives on Professions Overview.
The Twelve at a Glance
Your résumé grants two skills at T1 at creation — see Résumés. Every skill you haven't trained is T0, untrained.
Advancement

Advancing a skill is buying the next die — tiers are bought in order, one at a time, through study and practice in downtime. Learning is paid in LP earned over study periods; the same economy prices pattern practice (see Patterns).

content.label: DESIGN GAP
items: Skill training costs are unprinted. The check-system doc carries the learning (LP) economy “unchanged” from pre-rebuild Stat Architecture v1, but no rebuild source restates its numbers — credits, LP totals, study periods, and access requirements per tier are all unspecified.
Whether the unpicked feature of a tier's pair can ever be acquired later is unstated.

content.text: Sentinel Systems Career Services reminds contractors that training is the only investment that cannot be confiscated at a checkpoint. Schedule your next certification today. Bring credits.
the one true sentence sentinel ever printed.
The Skills
Each skill below lists its tier features. Effects are exact; anything a feature references by name — conditions, Charge, Leverage, nodes — lives on the page that owns it.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: Check System v1 calls for a DC calibration table on every skill (“Medicine — stabilize: Standard; field surgery: Hard”) so difficulties snap to the ladder instead of being invented. Only that illustrative Medicine example exists in the rebuild sources; the twelve calibration tables are unwritten.
Tech
Tech rolls Interface. Machines, repair, electronics — field fixes, downtime bench work, getting machines open and keeping them running.

rows: Analyze constructs and vehicles at no AP cost.
Improvised repairs without tools are possible, at +1 DC.
Locks and vehicles: a partial success gets you in — with noise.
Use Tech in place of Hacking against machines you have physical access to.
Once per mission, a device runs one tier above its rating for a scene, then breaks.
Your field repairs never trigger the permanent-consequence roll.

content.text: Hacking
Hacking rolls Interface. Intrusion, data, and EW gear — corp locks, data taps, access contests.

rows: Intrusions that land at partial or better leave no log.
Full intrusion successes also grab adjacent data.
Trigger Lock effects you deliver through gear last +1 round.
One free node Tag per mission from data taps.
Once per mission, win an access contest outright against a non-elite system.

content.text: Medicine
Medicine rolls Interface. Triage, treatment, the body — stabilizing the downed (see The Staircase), scar treatment, antidotes.

rows: Once per fight, stabilize at Close — the thrown kit.
Splice complications under your care downgrade one band.
You treat severe scars without elite facilities. The funnel's top, carried in two hands.

content.text: befriend whoever trains medicine. befriend them hard.
Negotiate
Negotiate rolls Edge. Deals, terms, the table — contracts, trades, and the Leverage that moves them (see Leverage & Encounter Play).

rows: Any full social success also reveals one Leverage tag.
Cold contacts start one disposition step warmer.
Buy at −10%, sell at +10%, with willing traders.
Once per scene, create a temporary Leverage tag from established fiction.
Once per mission, a witnessed partial social success becomes full. Deals you shake on stick.

content.text: Awareness
Awareness rolls Edge. Perception and the field — scouting, ambushes, reading a fight before it starts. The Analyze action lives on Turn, AP & Reactions.

rows: Analyze is free once per fight.
You are never ambushed at rest.
Scout from elevation or distance at advantage.
You know when your node is being watched.
At a fight's start, learn the enemy's tier and role composition.
You always know which cluster flanks you.
Once per mission, the GM warns you before a trap or ambush springs.

content.text: Athletics
Athletics rolls Chrome. The body in motion — climbing, grappling, shoving, falling well. The grapple contest itself lives on Turn, AP & Reactions.

rows: Once per fight, one free band move.
Falls of 2 bands or less cost you nothing.
Double endurance in chases, holds, and sprints.
Once per mission, auto-full any Athletics Check you could plausibly attempt.

content.text: Rift Lore
Rift Lore rolls Interface. Patterns, fauna, the other side. Weave Checks roll Rift Lore dice plus Interface — see Weave & Fire.

rows: Identify patterns and active weaves on sight.
Know a creature's taxonomy and Break line on sight.
Your Weave partials skip the +1 Charge.
New patterns cost −25% license and LP.
Null fields don't surcharge your rounds.
Once per arc, improvise a one-use pattern variant; the GM prices it.

content.text: Stealth
Stealth rolls Reflex. The approach — scouting, entries, the ambush round. Stealth is a phase, not a state: it ends at initiative (see Bands & Movement).

rows: Dome crowds hide you; pursuit rolls at disadvantage.
Your squad's ambush-round attacks gain +1 accuracy.
Your route leaves nothing for pursuers.
Once per mission, your squad takes a free ambush round entering a node — scouted or not.

content.text: Streetwise
Streetwise rolls Edge. The margin's operating system — rumors, fences, bought intel, knowing a guy.

rows: District travel never eats extra downtime periods.
Open a negotiation with anyone reachable.
One faction grants you Established-rank access.
Once per mission, you know a guy. The GM supplies the contact.

content.text: Resilience
Resilience rolls Grit. What the body endures — toxins, exhaustion, fear, the vent. Rattled and Seared live on Conditions; venting lives on Charge & Saturation.

rows: Advantage against toxins and bad rations.
Double shifts and forced marches, without complaint.
Once per fight, shed Rattled at the start of your activation.
Your Seared ticks are reduced by 1.
Once per fight, dropping below half grants 3 temp HP.
Your free vent is +1. Vent bonuses still cap at +2.
Exhaustion complications don't touch you, and once per mission a fear-class effect simply doesn't.

content.text: Harvesting
Harvesting rolls Grit. The carve — yields, grades, and the clocks they race (see Carving).

rows: Carving takes half the clock time.
Preservation clocks run ×1.5 in your care.
Combat-cracked shells skip the −1 grade under your knife.
Exact value and best buyer category, on sight.
Once per mission, one harvest upgrades a full grade.

content.text: Crafting
Crafting rolls Interface. The bench — mods, consumables, handloads, installs. Crafting at material cost is governed by The Material Cost Law.

rows: Identify mods and their quality on sight.
Craft Tier-1 consumables at half cost in downtime.
One extra install attempt per downtime.
Install your own mods at material cost.
Handload specialty rounds at material cost.
Once per arc, one item you craft gains a minor bespoke property; the GM prices it.
